rationale: Title X is more informative for JACC because it states the randomized comparison against medical therapy and gives the key 12-month recovery finding. Title Y has a more polished clinical-trial tone, but it is less specific about the comparator and softens the main result into a broader outcomes-through-12-months phrase.
loser_weakness: Title Y omits the medical-therapy comparator and uses somewhat redundant wording with transient and reversible, reducing specificity despite good journal fit.
